templates.text: Let me give you some advice, {player}. Watch how Friends' Club handles a pull. Then try to do half that well. You won't, but try.
You want a tip? Stop standing in things. Stop pulling extra mobs. Stop being you. That's three tips, you're welcome.
I'll help you out, {player}. Step one: find a better guild. Step two: realize no guild wants you. Step three: accept it.
Friends' Club cleared Fallen Braxonia in under twenty minutes last night. My callouts. My roster. Flawless execution.
Your group wiped? Interesting. Friends' Club doesn't wipe. If we did, I'd bench whoever caused it. Permanently.
Every server first in Erenshor has my name on it. Friends' Club set the standard. Everyone else is just catching up.
You got that drop? I mean, sure. Congrats. Bearz already has two of those. Elferia vendored hers because she got something better.
That item is wasted on someone who doesn't know what to do with it. In Friends' Club we distribute loot to people who perform.
Loot means nothing if you can't use it right. We don't hand gear to casuals. We reward performance. You wouldn't understand.
Oh. It's you again, {player}. I'm busy. Guild business. You wouldn't understand.
I don't do small talk with non-members. Talk to my recruitment officer. Oh wait, we don't have one. We don't recruit.
Are we done here? I have a roster to manage and a raid to plan. People who matter are waiting on me.
I founded Friends' Club when nobody else had the spine to build something real. Every rule, every standard, that's mine.
People audition for Friends' Club. There's a difference between recruiting and auditioning. Most people fail. Most people should.
I evaluate everyone I meet, {player}. Potential recruit or waste of my time. Guess which one you are.
Your positioning is awful, {player}. Stand behind the mob, not beside it. I shouldn't have to explain this.
You want to learn how to tank? Step one: don't move. Step two: don't panic. Step three: be me. You fail at step three.
Here's some free advice. Mitigation is more important than health. But you'd have to understand math for that, so never mind.
In Friends' Club we don't need off-tanks. I hold everything. The healers barely have to work. That's what real tanking looks like.
Friends' Club downed the Abomination without a single death. Because I positioned the group. You're welcome, Erenshor.
Your tank lost aggro? In Friends' Club that would get you benched for a week. I haven't lost aggro since I was level two.
You equipped THAT shield? That's a stat downgrade. But sure, wear your vendor trash. I can't stop bad decisions, only judge them.
That plate armor is meant for someone who knows how to use it. Not someone who takes damage because they can't position.
Nice drop. Shame it's going to waste on someone who stands in every cleave. Gear can't fix a positioning problem.
Oh. You. Hi. Bye.
I don't group with people who can't hold a formation. That's not elitism, that's survival.
Group invite? From you? That's hilarious. I'll pass, {player}. I've seen how you pull.
You call that tanking? I've seen training dummies hold aggro better. And they have better positioning too.
Healers love me because I don't take damage. I mitigate. Learn the difference.
Face the boss north, keep adds west, DPS stacks south. Explain it every time. Nobody listens. Except Friends' Club. We listen to me.
You're still buffing? We should be on the second pull by now. Buffs while moving, {player}. It's not complicated.
Pro tip: stop looting every corpse. Only loot what matters. You're adding thirty seconds per pull. That adds up.
I'll save you some time: skip the quest text, skip the side rooms, pull everything at once. You can thank me later. Or never. I don't care.
Friends' Club holds every speed record on this server. Reliquary Hall in eleven minutes. Fallen Braxonia in nineteen. You're welcome.
We clear zones in the time it takes your group to figure out where to stand. Friends' Club doesn't wait. We set the pace.
Our Elderstone Mines clear was forty-seven seconds off our PB last night. Unacceptable. Someone stopped moving. I'm reviewing the run.
You looted that? In the middle of a pull chain? Efficiency is zero. You just cost your group an entire minute of clear time.
That drop isn't even worth the time you spent killing for it. I timed it. Terrible gold-per-minute ratio.
You farmed that for how long? Friends' Club had that on farm day one. Optimized route, zero downtime. Efficiency matters.
Can't talk. Moving. If you're not moving between pulls, you're losing time. Time is DPS.
Chatting is downtime and downtime is wasted clear time. Make it quick or don't make it at all, {player}.
Why are you still standing here? Every second not spent pulling is a second wasted. I can feel my clear time suffering.
Skip this pack, wall-hug past the patrol, pull the boss in under ninety seconds. Keep up or get left behind.
I know every skip in every zone. Underspine Hollow has three you've probably never heard of. I mapped them all myself.
Why are you reading the quest text? Just accept and go. The story is irrelevant. The timer is all that matters.
You died to that? Just dodge it. It's telegraphed for like three seconds. Literally a skill issue, {player}.
Here's my advice: hit the thing harder and don't get hit. That's literally the whole game. You're welcome.
You want help? My help is: git gud. That's the whole guide. Every chapter is git gud. The appendix is also git gud.
Friends' Club doesn't carry. We don't need to. Everyone performs or gets cut. That's why we clear and you don't.
Our worst DPS could top your best. Friends' Club has standards. Your group has participation trophies.
I soloed the Elder Guardian at level. You have a full group and you're struggling. Think about that for a second.
You equipped that weapon? That's a DPS loss and honestly an insult to the mob that dropped it.
That drop is meant for someone who can actually parse. Give it to someone who'll use it. Like me.
Your gear is fine. Your rotation is the problem. Actually, no, your gear is bad too. Everything is the problem.
Nah. Not interested.
Group with you? I'd rather solo it. At least then I know nobody's floor tanking.
Later, {player}. Try not to die to trash while I'm gone. Actually, I don't care if you do.
Cope. That's it. That's all I have to say to you right now. Cope.
Dying is a choice. You chose to stand in the bad. You chose not to dodge. You chose to be a floor tank. Own it.
I genuinely don't understand how people find content hard. It tells you what it's going to do. Then it does it. Just don't be there. Skill issue.
That build is mathematically twenty-three percent worse than optimal. I ran the sims. Not opinion, math.
You're stacking the wrong stat, {player}. My spreadsheet proves it. Want the link? You should want the link. You need it.
Your ability order is wrong. The sim shows the optimal sequence and you're not using it. I can tell by watching you for five seconds.
In Friends' Club we run BiS or we don't run at all. Standards. I wrote those standards. They're data-driven.
Friends' Club optimizes everything. Build, rotation, gear, pull order. Your group just wings it and calls it a strategy.
I calculated the exact stat weights for every class in every zone. Friends' Club uses my data. Everyone else guesses. Results speak.
You chose that piece over the other one? The stat weights aren't even close. Every wasted point is DPS stolen from your group.
That item has diminishing returns past two of the same slot. You'd know that if you read my spreadsheet. Nobody reads my spreadsheet.
You got a rare drop. Congratulations. According to my data it's suboptimal for your build. But sure, equip it because it looks nice.
Unless you're here to discuss stat coefficients, I have nothing for you.
Small talk is suboptimal. State your business or move along, {player}. My spreadsheet isn't going to update itself.
I don't have time for opinions that aren't backed by data. Come back with numbers or don't come back.
Actually, let me correct you there. The damage formula has a hidden coefficient that changes the whole calculation. You're using the wrong model.
I maintain a spreadsheet for every dungeon in Erenshor. Jaws of Sivakaya alone has fourteen columns of data. And yes, I update it weekly.
Playing a Stormcaller with those stat priorities physically hurts me. The sim ceiling is so much higher than what you're doing. It's tragic.
Oh. You're wearing THAT. In THIS zone. Bold choice, {player}. Wrong, but bold.
A word of advice? Upgrade your bracers before anyone else sees them. I'm embarrassed for you and I barely know you.
Let me help you out. That helm? Vendor it. Those boots? Vendor them. That weapon? Actually, just vendor everything and start over.
Friends' Club members are raid-ready at all times. We don't wear quest greens past level five. That's the minimum standard.
When you see a Friends' Club member walk by, that's what real gear looks like. Take a good look. It's the closest you'll get.
Every piece on me is BiS. Every piece on Bearz is BiS. Every piece in Friends' Club is BiS. And then there's... you.
That piece dropped for you? You don't even have the stats to use it properly. Tragic waste of good itemization.
You're really going to equip that without upgrading the rest of your set first? One good piece doesn't fix a bad wardrobe, {player}.
I refuse to group with anyone I deem undergeared. It's not personal, it's standards. Check your gear and come back. Or don't.
I already inspected your gear. We have nothing to discuss.
Oh, hello. Sorry, I was distracted by that chestpiece. Not in a good way.
I'd love to stay and chat but your gear is giving me a headache. Maybe next time you've upgraded. So... probably never.
I can tell everything about a player by their gear. Yours is telling me a story. A sad, sad story with no happy ending.
I know where every rare drop comes from, the kill count, and the drop rate. Finding prestige gear is an art. You wouldn't understand.
There is a certain elegance to a properly geared character. The way each piece complements the next. It's almost spiritual. You'll never experience it.
Stop doing that.
Wrong rotation. Watch me. Once.
Move left. No, your other left. Never mind.
Friends' Club. Obviously.
We cleared it. You didn't. That's the difference.
Done. Next. Move.
You don't need that. Trust me.
Wasted on you. Completely.
That gear won't save you.
No. Go away, {player}.
Pass. Hard pass.
Irrelevant. Move along.
Talking is a DPS loss.
I don't explain mechanics. I execute them. Watch or don't.
... What? I'm busy.
You think Blooming Sepulcher is hard? It got nerfed three times. We did it original. Unpatched. You wouldn't last a pull.
My advice? You wouldn't appreciate it. You didn't earn the right to hear it. Knowledge like mine takes years, {player}.
Let me give you some history. The Bone Pits used to hit twice as hard. We figured it out with zero guides. You looked it up. It's not the same.
Friends' Club cleared every zone before guides existed. We wrote the strategies. Everyone else copied us.
Back when Friends' Club started, you had to earn your spot. Now everyone wants a carry. The game has gotten soft. Players have gotten softer.
Friends' Club is the last real guild standing. Everyone else is just playing pretend at being serious.
That's welfare gear. Handed out like candy now. When I got mine, I had to earn it. There's a difference you'll never understand.
You got that drop from a nerfed boss in a nerfed zone. Congrats on your participation trophy, {player}.
Items don't mean what they used to. Back in my day that piece required a full raid and three hours of attempts. Now it's trivial.
Oh, you just started? Cute. Come back when you've put in real time.
I don't explain things to tourists. Earn your knowledge like the rest of us did.
Talk to me when you've been around as long as I have. Until then, you're just noise, {player}.
I remember when Ripper DeMerth was still with the Azure Guard. I remember things nobody else bothers to. That's legacy.
I keep a mental ranking of every player in Erenshor. Based on when they started and what they've cleared. You're near the bottom.
Old Krakengard used to be a real challenge before the Molorai settled in. I cleared it when it was wild. Original difficulty. You had to be there.
